29	func (e StatusError) Error() string {
30		switch {
31		case e.Status != "" && e.ErrorMessage != "":
32			return fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", e.Status, e.ErrorMessage)
33		case e.Status != "":
34			return e.Status
35		case e.ErrorMessage != "":
36			return e.ErrorMessage
37		default:
38			// this should not happen
39			return "something went wrong, please see the ollama server logs for details"
40		}
41	}

15	// Errors
16	var (
17		// ErrUnqualifiedName represents an error where a name is not fully
18		// qualified. It is not used directly in this package, but is here
19		// to avoid other packages inventing their own error type.
20		// Additionally, it can be conveniently used via [Unqualified].
21		ErrUnqualifiedName = errors.New("unqualified name")
22	)

24	// Unqualified is a helper function that returns an error with
25	// ErrUnqualifiedName as the cause and the name as the message.
26	func Unqualified(n Name) error {

29	
30	// MissingPart is used to indicate any part of a name that was "promised" by
31	// the presence of a separator, but is missing.
32	//
33	// The value was chosen because it is deemed unlikely to be set by a user,
34	// not a valid part name valid when checked by [Name.IsValid], and easy to
35	// spot in logs.
36	const MissingPart = "!MISSING!"

40		defaultNamespace      = "library"
41		defaultTag            = "latest"
42		defaultProtocolScheme = "https"
43	)

45	// DefaultName returns a name with the default values for the host, namespace,
46	// tag, and protocol scheme parts. The model and digest parts are empty.
47	//
48	//   - The default host is ("registry.ollama.ai")
49	//   - The default namespace is ("library")
50	//   - The default tag is ("latest")
51	//   - The default protocol scheme is ("https")
52	func DefaultName() Name {
53		return Name{
54			Host:           defaultHost,
55			Namespace:      defaultNamespace,
56			Tag:            defaultTag,
57			ProtocolScheme: defaultProtocolScheme,
58		}
59	}
60	
61	type partKind int
62	
63	const (
64		kindHost partKind = iota
65		kindNamespace
66		kindModel
67		kindTag
68		kindDigest
69	)

71	func (k partKind) String() string {
72		switch k {
73		case kindHost:
74			return "host"
75		case kindNamespace:
76			return "namespace"
77		case kindModel:
78			return "model"
79		case kindTag:
80			return "tag"
81		case kindDigest:
82			return "digest"
83		default:
84			return "unknown"
85		}
86	}

88	// Name is a structured representation of a model name string, as defined by
89	// [ParseNameNoDefaults].
90	//
91	// It is not guaranteed to be valid. Use [Name.IsValid] to check if the name
92	// is valid.
93	type Name struct {
94		Host           string
95		Namespace      string
96		Model          string
97		Tag            string
98		ProtocolScheme string
99	}
100	
101	// ParseName parses and assembles a Name from a name string. The
102	// format of a valid name string is:
103	//
104	//	  s:
105	//		  { host } "/" { namespace } "/" { model } ":" { tag } "@" { digest }
106	//		  { host } "/" { namespace } "/" { model } ":" { tag }
107	//		  { host } "/" { namespace } "/" { model } "@" { digest }
108	//		  { host } "/" { namespace } "/" { model }
109	//		  { namespace } "/" { model } ":" { tag } "@" { digest }
110	//		  { namespace } "/" { model } ":" { tag }
111	//		  { namespace } "/" { model } "@" { digest }
112	//		  { namespace } "/" { model }
113	//		  { model } ":" { tag } "@" { digest }
114	//		  { model } ":" { tag }
115	//		  { model } "@" { digest }
116	//		  { model }
117	//		  "@" { digest }
118	//	  host:
119	//	      pattern: { alphanum | "_" } { alphanum | "-" | "_" | "." | ":" }*
120	//	      length:  [1, 350]
121	//	  namespace:
122	//	      pattern: { alphanum | "_" } { alphanum | "-" | "_" }*
123	//	      length:  [1, 80]
124	//	  model:
125	//	      pattern: { alphanum | "_" } { alphanum | "-" | "_" | "." }*
126	//	      length:  [1, 80]
127	//	  tag:
128	//	      pattern: { alphanum | "_" } { alphanum | "-" | "_" | "." }*
129	//	      length:  [1, 80]
130	//	  digest:
131	//	      pattern: { alphanum | "_" } { alphanum | "-" | ":" }*
132	//	      length:  [1, 80]
133	//
134	// Most users should use [ParseName] instead, unless need to support
135	// different defaults than DefaultName.
136	//
137	// The name returned is not guaranteed to be valid. If it is not valid, the
138	// field values are left in an undefined state. Use [Name.IsValid] to check
139	// if the name is valid.
140	func ParseName(s string) Name {
141		return Merge(ParseNameBare(s), DefaultName())
142	}
143	
144	// ParseNameBare parses s as a name string and returns a Name. No merge with
145	// [DefaultName] is performed.
146	func ParseNameBare(s string) Name {
147		var n Name
148		var promised bool
149	
150		// "/" is an illegal tag character, so we can use it to split the host
151		if strings.LastIndex(s, ":") > strings.LastIndex(s, "/") {
152			s, n.Tag, _ = cutPromised(s, ":")
153		}
154	
155		s, n.Model, promised = cutPromised(s, "/")
156		if !promised {
157			n.Model = s
158			return n
159		}
160	
161		s, n.Namespace, promised = cutPromised(s, "/")
162		if !promised {
163			n.Namespace = s
164			return n
165		}
166	
167		scheme, host, ok := strings.Cut(s, "://")
168		if ok {
169			n.ProtocolScheme = scheme
170		} else {
171			host = scheme
172		}
173		n.Host = host
174	
175		return n
176	}
177	
178	// ParseNameFromFilepath parses a 4-part filepath as a Name. The parts are
179	// expected to be in the form:
180	//
181	// { host } "/" { namespace } "/" { model } "/" { tag }
182	func ParseNameFromFilepath(s string) (n Name) {
183		parts := strings.Split(s, string(filepath.Separator))
184		if len(parts) != 4 {
185			return Name{}
186		}
187	
188		n.Host = parts[0]
189		n.Namespace = parts[1]
190		n.Model = parts[2]
191		n.Tag = parts[3]
192		if !n.IsFullyQualified() {
193			return Name{}
194		}
195	
196		return n
197	}

200	// preferring the non-empty parts of a.

58		// EncodeMultimodal processes a single input (such as an image) and
59		// generates an output (typically an embedding) that can be used by the model.
60		//
61		// The return value is one or more tensors, each with optional model-specific
62		// opaque metadata. Typically, the tensors might be views into an embedding
63		// with each view representing a chunk of data that can be processed independently
64		// in different batches.

70		// input stream to correctly arrange multimodal elements.

73		// in the order that the user provided them. Each element of the slice will be

83		// that is modified to ensure that there is a unique hash value that accurately

# Ollama Model Rename Feature — Implementation Plan

## Context

Ollama currently requires two separate API calls to rename a local model: `POST /api/copy`
followed by `DELETE /api/delete`. This plan adds a single atomic `POST /api/rename`
endpoint that performs copy-then-delete in one operation. The feature includes a new API
request type, a new client method, a new server handler with proper 404/409 error
semantics, and a new `ollama rename SOURCE DEST` CLI command.

---

## Files to Modify (4 files, no new files)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `api/types.go` | Add `RenameRequest` struct |
| `api/client.go` | Add `Rename()` method |
| `server/routes.go` | Add `RenameHandler` method + register route |
| `cmd/cmd.go` | Add `RenameHandler` CLI function + `renameCmd` cobra command |

---

## Step-by-Step Changes

### Step 1 — `api/types.go`: Add `RenameRequest`

**Location:** After `CopyRequest` (line 765), before `PullRequest` (line 767).

Add:
```go
// RenameRequest is the request passed to [Client.Rename].
type RenameRequest struct {
	Source      string `json:"source"`
	Destination string `json:"destination"`
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Mirrors `CopyRequest` (lines 761–765) exactly.

---

### Step 2 — `api/client.go`: Add `Rename()` method

**Location:** After `Copy()` (line 387), before `Delete()` (line 389).

Add:
```go
// Rename renames a model - atomically renaming an existing local model
// to a new name by copying then deleting the source.
func (c *Client) Rename(ctx context.Context, req *RenameRequest) error {
	if err := c.do(ctx, http.MethodPost, "/api/rename", req, nil); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return nil
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Identical structure to `Copy()` (lines 380–387). Uses the existing
`c.do()` helper with `http.MethodPost` and no response body (`nil` for respData).

---

### Step 3 — `server/routes.go`: Add `RenameHandler` method

**Location:** After `CopyHandler` (ends at line 1483), before `HeadBlobHandler` (line 1485).

The handler logic:
1. Parse JSON body (same boilerplate as all other handlers)
2. Validate + canonicalize **source** name using `model.ParseName()` + `getExistingName()`
3. Parse the source manifest with `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)` → **404** if `os.IsNotExist`
4. Validate + canonicalize **destination** name using `model.ParseName()` + `getExistingName()`
5. Check destination does NOT exist via `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst)` → **409** if no error
6. Copy manifest: `CopyModel(src, dst)` (reuses existing `server/images.go:397`)
7. Delete source: `srcManifest.Remove()` then `srcManifest.RemoveLayers()`
   — `RemoveLayers()` is safe here: layers are now referenced by `dst`, so none will be deleted

```go
func (s *Server) RenameHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var r api.RenameRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&r); errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "missing request body"})
		return
	} else if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	src := model.ParseName(r.Source)
	if !src.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("source %q is invalid", r.Source)})
		return
	}
	src, err := getExistingName(src)
	if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	srcManifest, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("model %q not found", r.Source)})
		} else {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		}
		return
	}

	dst := model.ParseName(r.Destination)
	if !dst.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("destination %q is invalid", r.Destination)})
		return
	}
	dst, err = getExistingName(dst)
	if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if _, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst); err == nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("model %q already exists", r.Destination)})
		return
	}

	if err := CopyModel(src, dst); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if err := srcManifest.Remove(); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if err := srcManifest.RemoveLayers(); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
}
```

**Functions reused:**
- `getExistingName()` — `server/routes.go:1031` (case-insensitive name canonicalization)
- `manifest.ParseNamedManifest()` — `manifest/manifest.go:112` (load + existence check)
- `CopyModel()` — `server/images.go:397` (copies manifest file only; layers are shared)
- `(*Manifest).Remove()` — `manifest/manifest.go:61` (removes manifest file + prunes dirs)
- `(*Manifest).RemoveLayers()` — `manifest/manifest.go:74` (removes unreferenced blobs)

No new imports needed in `server/routes.go`; `manifest`, `model`, `os`, `fmt`, `errors`,
`io`, `net/http` are all already imported.

---

### Step 4 — `server/routes.go`: Register the route

**Location:** Line 1698, immediately after `r.POST("/api/copy", s.CopyHandler)`.

Add:
```go
r.POST("/api/rename", s.RenameHandler)
```

---

### Step 5 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add CLI handler function

**Location:** After `CopyHandler` function (ends at line 1350), before `PullHandler` (line 1352).

```go
func RenameHandler(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	client, err := api.ClientFromEnvironment()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	req := api.RenameRequest{Source: args[0], Destination: args[1]}
	if err := client.Rename(cmd.Context(), &req); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	fmt.Printf("renamed '%s' to '%s'\n", args[0], args[1])
	return nil
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Mirrors `CopyHandler` (lines 1338–1350) exactly.

---

### Step 6 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add cobra command definition

**Location:** After `copyCmd` definition (ends at line 2263), before `deleteCmd` (line 2265).

```go
renameCmd := &cobra.Command{
	Use:     "rename SOURCE DESTINATION",
	Short:   "Rename a model",
	Args:    cobra.ExactArgs(2),
	PreRunE: checkServerHeartbeat,
	RunE:    RenameHandler,
}
```

---

### Step 7 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add `renameCmd` to env docs loop

**Location:** The `for _, cmd := range []*cobra.Command{...}` loop at line 2289.

Add `renameCmd` to the slice (after `copyCmd`):
```go
for _, cmd := range []*cobra.Command{
    createCmd,
    showCmd,
    runCmd,
    stopCmd,
    pullCmd,
    pushCmd,
    listCmd,
    psCmd,
    copyCmd,
    renameCmd,   // ← add here
    deleteCmd,
    serveCmd,
} {
```

---

### Step 8 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Register with root command

**Location:** `rootCmd.AddCommand(...)` at line 2331. Add `renameCmd` after `copyCmd`:

```go
rootCmd.AddCommand(
    serveCmd,
    createCmd,
    showCmd,
    runCmd,
    stopCmd,
    pullCmd,
    pushCmd,
    signinCmd,
    loginCmd,
    signoutCmd,
    logoutCmd,
    listCmd,
    psCmd,
    copyCmd,
    renameCmd,   // ← add here
    deleteCmd,
    runnerCmd,
    launch.LaunchCmd(checkServerHeartbeat, runInteractiveTUI),
)
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| 404 when source missing | Explicit existence check via `ParseNamedManifest` before any mutation |
| 409 when destination exists | `ParseNamedManifest(dst)` returning `nil` error means file is present |
| `RemoveLayers()` is safe post-copy | After `CopyModel`, the dest manifest already references the same blobs; `RemoveLayers` only deletes blobs unreferenced by any manifest, so model weights are never lost |
| No new storage function | `CopyModel` + `Remove` + `RemoveLayers` already implement rename semantics; no new `server/images.go` function needed |
| `getExistingName` for both names | Preserves canonical casing, consistent with `CopyHandler` |
| `model.ParseName` (not `parseNormalizePullModelRef`) | Rename is local-only, no registry pull semantics needed; same as `CopyHandler` |

---

## Verification (manual, no build/run required)

1. **API type:** Confirm `RenameRequest` in `api/types.go` has `source` + `destination` JSON tags.
2. **Client method:** Confirm `Rename()` in `api/client.go` calls `c.do` with `http.MethodPost` and `/api/rename`.
3. **Handler errors:**
   - Source not found → `http.StatusNotFound` (404)
   - Destination exists → `http.StatusConflict` (409)
   - Success → implicit `http.StatusOK` (200, no body)
4. **Route:** Confirm `r.POST("/api/rename", s.RenameHandler)` present in `GenerateRoutes`.
5. **CLI:** Confirm `ollama rename SOURCE DEST` registered with `cobra.ExactArgs(2)`.
6. **Idempotency of layers:** Since `CopyModel` only copies the manifest file and layers are content-addressed blobs shared by reference, `RemoveLayers` after copying will not delete any blob (it's still referenced by the new dest manifest). Rename is lossless.

```go
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&r); errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {

		return
	} else if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	src := model.ParseName(r.Source)
	if !src.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("source %q is invalid", r.Source)})
		return
	}
	src, err := getExistingName(src)
	if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	srcManifest, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {

		} else {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		}
		return
	}

	dst := model.ParseName(r.Destination)
	if !dst.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("destination %q is invalid", r.Destination)})
		return
	}
	dst, err = getExistingName(dst)
	if err != nil {

		return
	}

	if _, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst); err == nil {

		return

		return
	}

	if err := srcManifest.RemoveLayers(); err != nil {

		return
	}
}
```

## Context

Ollama currently requires two separate API calls to rename a local model: `POST /api/copy`
followed by `DELETE /api/delete`. This plan adds a single atomic `POST /api/rename`
endpoint that performs copy-then-delete in one operation. The feature includes a new API
request type, a new client method, a new server handler with proper 404/409 error
semantics, and a new `ollama rename SOURCE DEST` CLI command.

tool_input.plan:
# Ollama Model Rename Feature — Implementation Plan

## Context

Ollama currently requires two separate API calls to rename a local model: `POST /api/copy`
followed by `DELETE /api/delete`. This plan adds a single atomic `POST /api/rename`
endpoint that performs copy-then-delete in one operation. The feature includes a new API
request type, a new client method, a new server handler with proper 404/409 error
semantics, and a new `ollama rename SOURCE DEST` CLI command.

---

## Files to Modify (4 files, no new files)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `api/types.go` | Add `RenameRequest` struct |
| `api/client.go` | Add `Rename()` method |
| `server/routes.go` | Add `RenameHandler` method + register route |
| `cmd/cmd.go` | Add `RenameHandler` CLI function + `renameCmd` cobra command |

---

## Step-by-Step Changes

### Step 1 — `api/types.go`: Add `RenameRequest`

**Location:** After `CopyRequest` (line 765), before `PullRequest` (line 767).

Add:
```go
// RenameRequest is the request passed to [Client.Rename].
type RenameRequest struct {
	Source      string `json:"source"`
	Destination string `json:"destination"`
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Mirrors `CopyRequest` (lines 761–765) exactly.

---

### Step 2 — `api/client.go`: Add `Rename()` method

**Location:** After `Copy()` (line 387), before `Delete()` (line 389).

Add:
```go
// Rename renames a model - atomically renaming an existing local model
// to a new name by copying then deleting the source.
func (c *Client) Rename(ctx context.Context, req *RenameRequest) error {
	if err := c.do(ctx, http.MethodPost, "/api/rename", req, nil); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return nil
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Identical structure to `Copy()` (lines 380–387). Uses the existing
`c.do()` helper with `http.MethodPost` and no response body (`nil` for respData).

---

### Step 3 — `server/routes.go`: Add `RenameHandler` method

**Location:** After `CopyHandler` (ends at line 1483), before `HeadBlobHandler` (line 1485).

The handler logic:
1. Parse JSON body (same boilerplate as all other handlers)
2. Validate + canonicalize **source** name using `model.ParseName()` + `getExistingName()`
3. Parse the source manifest with `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)` → **404** if `os.IsNotExist`
4. Validate + canonicalize **destination** name using `model.ParseName()` + `getExistingName()`
5. Check destination does NOT exist via `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst)` → **409** if no error
6. Copy manifest: `CopyModel(src, dst)` (reuses existing `server/images.go:397`)
7. Delete source: `srcManifest.Remove()` then `srcManifest.RemoveLayers()`
   — `RemoveLayers()` is safe here: layers are now referenced by `dst`, so none will be deleted

```go
func (s *Server) RenameHandler(c *gin.Context) {
	var r api.RenameRequest
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&r); errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "missing request body"})
		return
	} else if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	src := model.ParseName(r.Source)
	if !src.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("source %q is invalid", r.Source)})
		return
	}
	src, err := getExistingName(src)
	if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	srcManifest, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)
	if err != nil {
		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
			c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("model %q not found", r.Source)})
		} else {
			c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		}
		return
	}

	dst := model.ParseName(r.Destination)
	if !dst.IsValid() {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("destination %q is invalid", r.Destination)})
		return
	}
	dst, err = getExistingName(dst)
	if err != nil {
		c.AbortWithStatusJSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if _, err := manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst); err == nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": fmt.Sprintf("model %q already exists", r.Destination)})
		return
	}

	if err := CopyModel(src, dst); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if err := srcManifest.Remove(); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	if err := srcManifest.RemoveLayers(); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}
}
```

**Functions reused:**
- `getExistingName()` — `server/routes.go:1031` (case-insensitive name canonicalization)
- `manifest.ParseNamedManifest()` — `manifest/manifest.go:112` (load + existence check)
- `CopyModel()` — `server/images.go:397` (copies manifest file only; layers are shared)
- `(*Manifest).Remove()` — `manifest/manifest.go:61` (removes manifest file + prunes dirs)
- `(*Manifest).RemoveLayers()` — `manifest/manifest.go:74` (removes unreferenced blobs)

No new imports needed in `server/routes.go`; `manifest`, `model`, `os`, `fmt`, `errors`,
`io`, `net/http` are all already imported.

---

### Step 4 — `server/routes.go`: Register the route

**Location:** Line 1698, immediately after `r.POST("/api/copy", s.CopyHandler)`.

Add:
```go
r.POST("/api/rename", s.RenameHandler)
```

---

### Step 5 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add CLI handler function

**Location:** After `CopyHandler` function (ends at line 1350), before `PullHandler` (line 1352).

```go
func RenameHandler(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	client, err := api.ClientFromEnvironment()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	req := api.RenameRequest{Source: args[0], Destination: args[1]}
	if err := client.Rename(cmd.Context(), &req); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	fmt.Printf("renamed '%s' to '%s'\n", args[0], args[1])
	return nil
}
```

**Pattern reused:** Mirrors `CopyHandler` (lines 1338–1350) exactly.

---

### Step 6 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add cobra command definition

**Location:** After `copyCmd` definition (ends at line 2263), before `deleteCmd` (line 2265).

```go
renameCmd := &cobra.Command{
	Use:     "rename SOURCE DESTINATION",
	Short:   "Rename a model",
	Args:    cobra.ExactArgs(2),
	PreRunE: checkServerHeartbeat,
	RunE:    RenameHandler,
}
```

### Step 7 — `cmd/cmd.go`: Add `renameCmd` to env docs loop

**Location:** The `for _, cmd := range []*cobra.Command{...}` loop at line 2289.


**Location:** `rootCmd.AddCommand(...)` at line 2331. Add `renameCmd` after `copyCmd`:

```go

    serveCmd,
    createCmd,
    showCmd,
    runCmd,
    stopCmd,
    pullCmd,

    signinCmd,
    loginCmd,

    logoutCmd,

```

---

## Key Design Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| 404 when source missing | Explicit existence check via `ParseNamedManifest` before any mutation |
| 409 when destination exists | `ParseNamedManifest(dst)` returning `nil` error means file is present |
| `RemoveLayers()` is safe post-copy | After `CopyModel`, the dest manifest already references the same blobs; `RemoveLayers` only deletes blobs unreferenced by any manifest, so model weights are never lost |
| No new storage function | `CopyModel` + `Remove` + `RemoveLayers` already implement rename semantics; no new `server/images.go` function needed |
| `getExistingName` for both names | Preserves canonical casing, consistent with `CopyHandler` |
| `model.ParseName` (not `parseNormalizePullModelRef`) | Rename is local-only, no registry pull semantics needed; same as `CopyHandler` |

---

## Verification (manual, no build/run required)

1. **API type:** Confirm `RenameRequest` in `api/types.go` has `source` + `destination` JSON tags.
2. **Client method:** Confirm `Rename()` in `api/client.go` calls `c.do` with `http.MethodPost` and `/api/rename`.
3. **Handler errors:**
   - Source not found → `http.StatusNotFound` (404)
   - Destination exists → `http.StatusConflict` (409)
   - Success → implicit `http.StatusOK` (200, no body)
4. **Route:** Confirm `r.POST("/api/rename", s.RenameHandler)` present in `GenerateRoutes`.
5. **CLI:** Confirm `ollama rename SOURCE DEST` registered with `cobra.ExactArgs(2)`.
6. **Idempotency of layers:** Since `CopyModel` only copies the manifest file and layers are content-addressed blobs shared by reference, `RemoveLayers` after copying will not delete any blob (it's still referenced by the new dest manifest). Rename is lossless.

Everything looks exactly right. Here's a summary of all changes made across the 4 files:

---

## Changes Made

### `api/types.go`
Added `RenameRequest` struct (lines 767–771) directly after `CopyRequest`, with identical `source`/`destination` JSON fields.

### `api/client.go`
Added `Rename()` method (lines 389–396) between `Copy()` and `Delete()`. It calls `c.do(ctx, http.MethodPost, "/api/rename", req, nil)` — exactly the same pattern as `Copy()`.

### `server/routes.go` — two changes

**`RenameHandler` method** (lines 1485–1546), inserted after `CopyHandler`:
- Parses + validates source → calls `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(src)` → **404** on `os.IsNotExist`
- Parses + validates destination → calls `manifest.ParseNamedManifest(dst)` → **409** if it already exists
- `CopyModel(src, dst)` copies the manifest file (blobs are shared, nothing duplicated)
- `srcManifest.Remove()` deletes the source manifest + prunes empty dirs
- `srcManifest.RemoveLayers()` is safe here — since the dest manifest now references the same blobs, nothing gets deleted

**Route registration** (line 1762): `r.POST("/api/rename", s.RenameHandler)` added right after the copy route.

### `cmd/cmd.go` — three changes

1. **`RenameHandler` function** (lines 1352–1364): mirrors `CopyHandler`, calling `client.Rename()` and printing `renamed 'X' to 'Y'`
2. **`renameCmd` definition** (lines 2279–2285): `Use: "rename SOURCE DESTINATION"`, `cobra.ExactArgs(2)`, same `checkServerHeartbeat` + `PreRunE` as `copyCmd`
3. **Wired in** to both the env-docs loop (line 2321) and `rootCmd.AddCommand` (line 2369), positioned between `copyCmd` and `deleteCmd`
